message:
fix(sync): refresh queued waker on re-poll in OnceCell::get_or_init

When a task waiting on OnceCell initialization is re-polled with a
different waker (e.g., after being moved between executors or when the
runtime reassigns wakers), the queued waiter entry previously retained
the original stale waker. When the initializing task completed or was
cancelled, the stale waker was notified instead of the current one,
leaving the waiting task stranded.

Now the Some(flag) branch in poll_waiter finds the existing waiter entry
by Arc pointer equality and refreshes its waker via clone_from, ensuring
the latest waker is always notified on state transitions.

Added CountWaker test helper that tracks wake invocations, and a test
(get_or_init_waiter_refreshes_queued_waker) that:
  1. Starts a pending initialization (Task A)
  2. Queues a waiter (Task B) with waker_a
  3. Re-polls Task B with waker_b
  4. Drops Task A to trigger waiter notification
  5. Asserts waker_b was notified, waker_a was not

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
